perturbation.original_text: 3. Disclosure: A party (the “ disclosing Party ”) may, in connection with the Business Discussions, disclose to the other Party informatio n, including, without limitation, oral, written, graphical or electronic information and all compilations, analyses, notes, copies prepared, generated or delivered ther e from, which is identified as “confidential information” or “confidential busines s information” (the “Confidential Business Information ”) by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure to the receiving party (the “ Receiving Party ”). The fact the Business Discussions are occurring and any negotiations, ter ms or facts related to the Business Discussions are Confidential Business Info rmation.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Disclosure: A party (the “ disclosing Party ”) may, in connection with the Business Discussions, disclose to the other Party information. The fact the Business Discussions are occurring and any negotiations, ter ms or facts related to the Business Discussions are Confidential Business Info rmation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'confidential information' or 'confidential business information' and the requirements for how it needs to be identified as such, introduces ambiguity. The receiving party could argue information shared was never labeled as 'confidential information', thus circumventing the agreement, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure: A party (the “ disclosing Party ”) may, in connection with the Business Discussions, disclose to the other Party information. The fact the Business Discussions are occurring and any negotiations, ter ms or facts related to the Business Discussions are Confidential Business Info rmation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the definition and identification process for 'Confidential Business Information'. The contradicted text, being identical to the changed text, relies on the existence of 'Confidential Business Information', which is no longer adequately defined in the same section. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now lacks a clear mechanism for identifying what constitutes confidential information, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality obligations, as information disclosed may no longer be reliably classified as confidential. The removal directly impacts the definition referenced and required for enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The restrictions as to the use and disclosure set o ut above shall not apply to: a. any of the confidential information which is or bec omes published otherwise than by unauthorised publication in breac h of this agreement. b. or any of the Confidential Information which is sho wn by written or other tangible evidence to have been known to the recipie nt prior to the date of the disclosure hereunder. c. or any of the confidential information which is law fully acquired by the recipient from an independent source having a bona fida right to disclose the same. d. or any Confidential Information which as demonstrat e by tangible evidence is independently developed by an employee of the re cipient who has not had access to any of the Information disclosed to the r ecipient by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The restrictions as to the use and disclosure set o ut above shall not apply to: a. any information which is or bec omes published. b. or any information which is sho wn by written or other tangible evidence to have been known to the recipie nt prior to the date of the disclosure hereunder. c. or any information which is law fully acquired by the recipient from an independent source. d. or any Information which as demonstrat e by tangible evidence is independently developed by an employee of the re cipient.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'of the Confidential Information' and 'having a bona fida right to disclose the same' and 'who has not had access to any of the Information disclosed to the r ecipient by the other party' in the exceptions, any information published, known beforehand, acquired from any independent source (regardless of right to disclose), or independently developed by an employee (even with access to the disclosed information) would be exempt. This greatly weakens the NDA's protections, creating uncertainty in what is actually protected. The fact that confidential information is mentioned elsewhere makes this a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The restrictions as to the use and disclosure set o ut above shall not apply to: a. any information which is or bec omes published. b. or any information which is sho wn by written or other tangible evidence to have been known to the recipie nt prior to the date of the disclosure hereunder. c. or any information which is law fully acquired by the recipient from an independent source. d. or any Information which as demonstrat e by tangible evidence is independently developed by an employee of the re cipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies exceptions to the confidentiality restrictions concerning 'Confidential Information' and requires 'bona fide right to disclose' and 'access to the Information'. The changed text broadens these exceptions by omitting 'of the Confidential Information', 'having a bona fide right to disclose the same' and 'who has not had access to any of the Information disclosed to the recipient by the other party', which means the exceptions now apply to any information regardless of its confidentiality status or whether the source had the right to disclose or access the information which directly contradicts the original intent to protect confidential information. This creates a significant contradiction because it undermines the scope of what is considered confidential and protected, making the terms inconsistent and unenforceable. The explanation further supports the presence of a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed text, which only highlights and confirms that there is a contradiction since it shows the changed text is contradicting the original intention of keeping certain things confidential.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Neither party shall, with out the prior written con sent of the other party, copy or reproduce any document which may be supplied hereun der and either party receiving any such document will a) return the same and any copies made thereof to the party supplying the same if so requested by the supplying party and b) certify in writing that it has complied with this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Neither party shall, with out the prior written con sent of the other party, copy or reproduce any document which may be supplied hereun der and either party receiving any such document will a) return the same and any copies made thereof to the party supplying the same if so requested by the supplying party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'and b) certify in writing that it has complied with this agreement' from the obligations of the receiving party. Now there isn't an obligation from the reciving party to certify in writing that it has complied with this agreement, making enforcement of the document return clause difficult. The fact that the agreement mentions compliance elsewhere makes this an contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party shall, with out the prior written con sent of the other party, copy or reproduce any document which may be supplied hereun der and either party receiving any such document will a) return the same and any copies made thereof to the party supplying the same if so requested by the supplying party.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the receiving party to both return the documents and certify in writing that they have complied with the agreement. The changed text removes the requirement to certify compliance. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Removing the certification requirement makes it difficult to enforce the document return clause because there's no written confirmation of compliance, which creates a contradiction as mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The confidential information, including any documen ts, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, w ill remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no right s are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not except the limite d right to use set out above with respect to the said Field.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The confidential information, including any documen ts, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, w ill remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no right s are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except the limite d right to use set out above with respect to the said Field', this changes the right to use. Originally the right to use was limited to the said field, but now with its removal, the right to use has no stated limits, potentially allowing its use in other fields. The limited use set out in the agreement now doesn't exit, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information, including any documen ts, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, w ill remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no right s are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 'limited right to use' the confidential information 'with respect to the said Field'. The changed text removes this limitation, granting a potentially unlimited right to use the confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'no rights are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not'. The removal of the limitation creates a right where previously none existed, leading to a clear contradiction.
